wins: orchestrator: Coordinate the active-duty roster, daily question email path, and mobile rollout sequencing
frontend: Refine the premium hero, background linework, and logo presentation so the product feels more premium than Archer or UWorld
backend: Design the leanest daily question email capture and delivery path without adding fragile complexity
social-studio: Run a warm reply wave on CCRN, CRNA-school, and ICU-study posts
blockers: manager: beacon: Resend is selected, but CHAPAI_EMAIL_API_KEY or RESEND_API_KEY is still missing.
lessons: Promote only outcome-level memory when a lane actually ships or changes focus.
Human blockers should stay compressed to one exact action, not a broad status paragraph.
Sleeping lanes should carry a clear next wake condition instead of masquerading as active work.
handoffs: Manager should keep the next bounded owner visible for each active lane.
Blocked lanes should emit the shortest direct human unblock and then stop writing noise.
Scout and explorer should refresh only when new validation work exists or a handoff changes.
next: Clear the top external block only after all automated retries are exhausted.
Keep Nemoclaw compounding cheap mixed batches.
Let manager, Gemini, and Antigravity keep the cram and conversion lanes tight.

agentCloseouts.shipped: Reviewed 9 frontpage concepts (1301-1309), 50 logo options (1101-1150), and the live BrandMark component. The homepage hero structure is typographically clean and the parchment palette is correct, but the right-side illustrations do not read as anatomical plates Î“Ã‡Ã¶ they are abstract topographic blobs. The logo batch is almost entirely color-variation exhaustion of two mark types (NEURAL ARC and HEARTLINE), producing no new mark geometry. BrandMark.tsx is a dead `` tag with no live SVG mark at all. The system is not yet premium medical illustration; it is still symbolic medical vector art with editorial typography layered on top.
